title: echium parviflorum var. vivianii
intro: Echium parviflorum var. vivianii, also known as the Azores blue, is a subspecies of the Echium parviflorum plant, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the borage family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the production of nitric oxide, which can help to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Echium parviflorum var. vivianii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and act as an antioxidant, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as an herbal supplement, typically in the form of capsules or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including hypertension and cardiovascular disease.
